name: General Motors
headline: General Motors FIRST-BASELINE CONFIRMATION at 40.6 / Functional (0.6pt above Developing floor). Detroit AI-substitution layoff cluster (~500-600 IT workers May 11-12, Austin and Warren) with scripted-HR-video terminations and no acknowledgment of impact; broader Detroit Three context (20,000+ salaried jobs cut, ~19% of combined workforce). AI-native-roles backfill underway. The conduct pattern (scripted-video layoff with 'no appreciation or empathy' per laid-off worker testimony) is an EMP-dimension signal at boundary-sensitive position. Confirm at 40.6 with explicit boundary flag — if next-cycle evidence (additional layoff wave, worker-welfare reporting, or industry-cluster escalation) materializes, downgrade trajectory is live.

AWR.rationale: Sustained. Internal workforce-displacement awareness present; HR pre-planned scripts demonstrate top-down awareness. Worker-side sense-making not surfaced.

EMP.rationale: Sustained at first-baseline. E1 affective resonance: scripted-video terminations described by laid-off workers as having 'no appreciation or empathy. No questions. Nothing.' (CNBC May 12). This is anchor-2 evidence ('occasional acknowledgment, no structural expectation') trending toward anchor-1. Carry-forward note: a quarter-step EMP downgrade may be warranted if next cycle confirms pattern continuation.

ACT.rationale: Sustained. Severance and benefit packages provided; standard sector response. AC2 proportionality of severance to worker displacement is not independently audited.

BND.rationale: Sustained. B3 scope clarity: GM communicated AI-pivot intent publicly; layoff timing aligns with announced strategy. B5 consent-orientation issues (scripted-video with no Q&A pathway) are concerning but not a full BND downgrade trigger.

ACC.rationale: Sustained. No regulatory or oversight finding against GM's layoff process; sector-relative severance package within norms.

SYS.rationale: Sustained. S1 root-cause-orientation: AI-substitution is symptom of broader software-defined-vehicle restructuring, not isolated event. S5 coalitional-compassion: no sector-collaborative workforce-transition program documented.

INT.rationale: Sustained. I3 internal-consistency: scripted-video terminations + stated commitments to AI workforce transition show partial alignment but the EMP gap (worker testimony of no empathy) is the visible inconsistency. I4 values-alignment: layoff timing during 90% AI-generated code milestone is values-consistent with stated AI strategy but raises the question of whether values include worker-welfare considerations.

evidence.finding: Laid-off GM employees describe scripted-video terminations with 'no appreciation or empathy. No questions. Nothing.' Severance packages provided.
Detroit Three (GM, Ford, Stellantis) have together cut more than 20,000 US salaried jobs (~19% of combined workforces) from recent peaks; AI displacement framed explicitly in sector reporting.
GM laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with AI skills; strategic skills swap rather than headcount reduction.
GM CEO Mary Barra: 90% of autonomous-driving software now AI-generated.
Up to 600 white-collar jobs being cut while GM continues hiring AI-focused technical roles.

keyEvidence: First-baseline assessment at canonical 40.6 (0.6pt above Functional floor).
500-600 IT layoffs (Austin TX, Warren MI) May 11-12 with scripted-video terminations.
Worker testimony: 'No appreciation or empathy. No questions. Nothing.' (CNBC May 12).
Detroit Three cluster: 20,000+ US salaried jobs cut (~19% combined workforce) recent peak.
AI-substitution framing: 90% of GM autonomous code now AI-generated.
Boundary case: 0.6pt above Functional/Developing floor — downgrade trajectory live if next-cycle evidence accumulates.
rationale: First-baseline confirmation at 40.6. The published score sits 0.6pt above the Functional/Developing floor (40.0). The May 11-12 layoff event and its worker-testimony EMP signal are sub-threshold negative on their own — not enough to justify a quarter-step EMP downgrade at first-baseline given that severance and benefits are within sector norms. The next cycle should monitor for: (a) additional layoff waves, (b) regulatory or congressional engagement, (c) industry-cluster escalation (Ford/Stellantis follow-on layoffs), and (d) any structured worker-transition program. Conservative direction protocol: at boundary, hold position unless evidence is unambiguous. Math: (2.5+2.5+3+2.5+3+2.5+2.5+2.5)/8 = 21/8 = 2.625 → ((2.625-1)/4)*100 = 40.625 → 40.6.
whyHeadline: Detroit AI-substitution layoffs land 0.6 points above Functional floor; scripted-video terminations flagged for next cycle.

dominantDimension.note: EMP is the dimension with the strongest negative signal (scripted-video terminations, 'no empathy' worker testimony) but the evidence is sub-threshold for a quarter-step downgrade at first-baseline.

nextForwardSignal.label: Next quarterly layoff wave (sector watch) and any worker-transition program announcement

watches: Ford and Stellantis follow-on layoffs (sector cluster escalation)
Congressional or DOL engagement on AI-substitution layoff transparency
Worker-transition or retraining program announcement (positive signal trigger)
carryForward: Scripted-video termination methodology candidate (sector-wide pattern emerging)
newConductCategoryNote: Methodology candidate: 'scripted-video-termination' as an EMP-dimension sub-anchor under E1 affective resonance. Distinguished from standard severance protocols by the absence of two-way interaction (no Q&A pathway), pre-scripted HR delivery, and worker testimony of 'no acknowledgment of impact.' GM May 11-12 is the canonical instance; if Ford and Stellantis follow the same protocol, sector-wide methodology formalization should be considered. Tier-3 evidence (worker testimony via press).
boundaryWatchResolution: Boundary case. 40.6 sits 0.6pt above Developing/Functional floor (40/41). Per conservative-direction protocol, downgrade calls move toward less-favorable Developing band. The May 11-12 evidence is real but sub-threshold for a full quarter-step EMP downgrade alone. Hold position; escalate watch to weekly during the AI-pivot-displacement-wave sector cycle.

math_check.validator_check: Math clean. First-baseline confirmation at boundary-sensitive position.

disclaimer: This assessment is based on publicly available information and does not constitute a formal Compassion Benchmark Certified Assessment.
